title: Unsanitized superglobal input
summary: WordPress.org review and security best practices expect request data from superglobals to be unslashed and sanitized before use.

detection.notes: Current repo coverage is grep-based and does not perform data-flow analysis. Official static analysis should remain part of the workflow.

guidance.why_it_matters: Using raw request data can lead to security issues and is one of the most common WordPress.org review blockers.
guidance.fix: Use isset() checks, wp_unslash() where needed, and context-appropriate sanitization functions before processing request values.
guidance.false_positive_risk: High. Naive text matching cannot reliably determine whether sanitization happens earlier or later in the flow.
